en.title: Yes, verified. The LUCAS (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System) is a real US one-way loitering mun
en.desc: Yes, verified. The LUCAS (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System) is a real US one-way loitering munition, reverse-engineered from Iran's Shahed-136. CENTCOM confirmed its first combat use in Operation Epic Fury (Feb 28 strikes on Iran). Multiple outlets (TWZ, Military Times, Business Insider) and footage show locals in Iraq recovering a nearly intact crashed example, matching the delta-wing design with visible satellite antenna. The video in the original post depicts exactly that recovery.

en.desc: The clip captures Channel 4 reporter Secunder Kermani questioning Israeli Foreign Ministry spokesman Oren Marmorstein at the Tel Aviv site of Iran's recent missile barrage. Kermani acknowledges Iran's regime abuses but cites Netanyahu's ICC arrest warrant to argue Israel has "no moral high ground." Marmorstein responds by calling the Iranian strikes a war crime for deliberately targeting civilians (killing at least one elderly woman and her caregiver). It's a tense exchange reflecting broader Israel-Iran tensions post-recent strikes. Both sides accuse the other of crimes; facts show Iran's missiles hit populated areas while Israel's ops targeted military sites.

en.title: Reports from Iranian authorities, Al Jazeera, NYT, BBC, Reuters, and HRANA confirm civilian deaths i
en.desc:
Reports from Iranian authorities, Al Jazeera, NYT, BBC, Reuters, and HRANA confirm civilian deaths in US-Israeli strikes since Feb 28, including a girls' school strike in Minab killing ~165 (mostly children). Hospitals like Gandhi in Tehran sustained damage from nearby blasts and were evacuated; no verified direct hospital hits. 

Israel/US state all targets were military/nuclear/leadership sites amid Iran's missile responses. Total Iranian deaths reported 555-742, with collateral civilian impact on both sides.

en.desc: No, that's Iranian propaganda from official Ali Larijani. The post presents a selective, unverified narrative. 

The Feb 28 girls' school strike in Minab (Ira
en.desc:
The post presents a selective, unverified narrative. 

The Feb 28 girls' school strike in Minab (Iranian claims: 150-168 dead, mostly children) occurred near an IRGC base; Israel/US say they are investigating, have not confirmed responsibility. 

March 1 hospital damage in Tehran (e.g. Gandhi) reported by witnesses, amid broader strikes on military targets. 

No credible reports exist of a children's playground strike in Iran today. 

Strikes target Iranian regime military/nuclear sites after years of threats and proxy attacks; Iran has fired missiles killing civilians in Israel and Gulf states. Civilian casualties in war are tragic but require full context, not one-sided claims.

en.desc:
The video is from January 2020: drone footage of the massive funeral procession for Qasem Soleimani in Tehran after the US drone strike. It showed pro-regime crowds, not a recent anti-US/Israel event or response to Trump.

No credible reports of Iranians turning out in "record numbers against American & Israeli imperialism" in 2026. Recent Iranian elections have had historically low turnout (often under 40%, seen as regime rejection). Trump's past calls for Iranian uprising (e.g., 2020) didn't trigger this. The post misuses old footage.
